senses.definition: a person whose job is to talk on radio or TV programs or to present them

examples.x: The broadcaster delivered the evening news with great professionalism.
She became a well-known broadcaster after years of working in radio.
The broadcaster provided live updates from the scene of the event.
His role as a broadcaster includes interviewing guests and presenting news.
The broadcaster’s voice is familiar to many listeners in the area.

senses.definition: a radio or TV presenter who is in charge of giving information about different programs

examples.x: The announcer introduced the guest speakers at the event.
She worked as an announcer for the local radio station.
The announcer’s voice was clear and engaging during the live broadcast.
At the sports game, the announcer provided play-by-play commentary.
He started his career as an announcer before moving into television reporting.

senses.definition: someone who introduces news on a live TV or radio program by other broadcasters

examples.x: The anchor delivered the breaking news with a calm and authoritative demeanor.
She became a household name as the anchor of the primetime news program.
The network hired a seasoned journalist to serve as the anchor for its flagship morning show.
The anchor kept viewers informed during the storm with updates from the field reporters.
After decades in the industry, he retired as one of the most respected anchors in broadcast journalism.

senses.definition: someone who is in charge of operating the camera in producing a TV program or a motion picture

examples.x: The camera operator zoomed in on the speaker as he began his important announcement.
During the concert, the camera operator moved quickly to capture the best shots of the performers.
The camera operator worked closely with the director to ensure the scene was filmed exactly as planned.
After a few years of practice, she became one of the best camera operators on the set.

senses.definition: a radio presenter that announces and plays recorded music

examples.x: The disk jockey played some classic rock songs that got everyone dancing at the party.
The radio station hired a new disk jockey to host the morning show.
She became a well-known disk jockey after years of working at local clubs and radio stations.
The disk jockey announced the upcoming tracks and interacted with listeners during the live broadcast.

senses.definition: a person who is in charge of managing the stage in a TV production

examples.x: The floor manager made sure the cameras were in position before the show started.
During the live broadcast, the floor manager gave the cues for when to switch to the next segment.
As soon as the director gave the signal, the floor manager instructed the crew to move to the next scene.
The floor manager is responsible for keeping track of time during the live broadcast to avoid delays.
In the studio, the floor manager made sure all the microphones were working before the program went live.

senses.definition: a person, particularly of a well-known status, who has been asked to temporarily take part in a program, like a television show, concert, etc.

examples.x: The famous actor was a guest on last night's chat show.
The concert featured a surprise guest who delighted the audience.
She was thrilled to be invited as a guest on the popular morning talk show.
The TV host welcomed the celebrated author as their special guest.
The band's guest singer performed a stunning solo during the concert.

senses.definition: the person in front of a camera who talks about different topics or invites guests to a TV or radio show

examples.x: The host skillfully navigated through diverse topics on the evening talk show.
She earned acclaim as the charismatic host of a popular morning news program.
The host introduced the celebrity guest and asked insightful questions.
As a radio show host, he brought humor and wit to every broadcast.
The host's engaging personality kept the audience tuned in for the entire hour.

senses.definition: a presenter who reads the news during a TV or radio program

examples.x: The newscaster delivered breaking news updates throughout the day.
She aspired to become a newscaster to inform the public about important issues.
Viewers rely on the newscaster for accurate reporting on current events.
The seasoned newscaster anchored the evening news with authority and clarity.
The newscaster reported on local events with empathy and insight.

senses.definition: a person who deals with supervisory tasks or financial affairs in making a motion picture, play, etc.

examples.x: The producer secured the funding needed to start filming the movie.
She worked as the producer for several successful TV series.
The producer's role is crucial in coordinating the various elements of the play.
He became a famous producer known for his blockbuster films.
The producer handled all the logistical details of the theater production.

senses.definition: a male TV presenter whose job involves telling the audience what is happening in different sections of a TV program
a person who provides a spoken commentary for a TV show, movie, etc. whom the audience cannot see
a person or an organization that pays the expenses of a TV, radio or online program as a means of advertisement
a performer who provides voices for animated films, TV shows, video games, commercials, audiobooks, and other media where speaking voices are needed
a radio presenter who is known for expressing provocative or offensive opinions

examples.x: The shock jock's attempt to joke about a serious event left many listeners offended.
Some people find shock jocks funny, while others feel they go too far with their humor.
The radio station had to issue an apology after the shock jock made an insensitive remark on air.
The shock jock's comments about politics sparked a lot of controversy on social media.

senses.definition: a scientist who studies and predicts weather conditions by analyzing atmospheric patterns, utilizing tools such as weather models, instruments, and data to provide forecasts and weather-related information

examples.x: The meteorologist on the news predicted rain for the weekend.
I watched the meteorologist explain the severe storm on TV this morning.
The meteorologist uses satellites to track hurricanes as they form.
Our local meteorologist warned about icy roads during the morning commute.
She became a meteorologist because she loves studying the weather.

senses.definition: an individual who watches content, such as videos, TV programs, or live streams, through traditional broadcasting channels or digital platforms

examples.x: The TV network aims to captivate viewers with compelling storytelling and high-quality production in its programming.
As a loyal viewer of the show, Sarah eagerly anticipates each new episode and enjoys discussing it with friends afterward.
Viewer feedback plays an essential role in shaping the direction of the network's programming, helping to ensure it resonates with its audience.
With the advent of streaming services, viewers now have greater control over what they watch and when, allowing for more personalized viewing experiences.

senses.definition: someone who listens to a radio program, often in a regular manner

examples.x: As a devoted listener, Sarah tunes in to her favorite radio show every morning during her commute to work.
The podcast host values feedback from listeners and often incorporates their suggestions into future episodes.
Radio stations strive to engage their listeners by playing a diverse range of music and hosting interactive segments.
The talk show's host has a loyal following of listeners who appreciate his insightful commentary on current events.
Streaming platforms track listener data to provide personalized recommendations based on individual preferences and listening habits.

senses.definition: a person who takes part in a discussion or debate on a TV or radio show, offering their opinions or expertise

examples.x: The panelist shared her views on climate change during the TV debate.
The show featured a panelist from each industry to discuss the future of work.
She was invited as a panelist to talk about the importance of mental health awareness.
As a panelist, he was asked to comment on the latest technology trends.

senses.definition: an individual who broadcasts or live streams content, such as video games, creative artwork, or other activities, over the internet through platforms like Twitch, YouTube, or similar platforms
